Here is the latest in tech today.
• Google is rebranding its NotebookLM app to Gemini Notebook
• Trump Media is selling early post access to Wall Street traders
• Wired looks at OpenAI's ongoing legal and reputational hurdles HEADLINNE.com #TechNews #Google

Here is today's top finance news

linkedin.title: This Week in Finance & Tech
linkedin.body:
It has been a busy week for major tech companies. Meta shares climbed on Thursday after the company announced a new AI model and made progress on its custom chip development. Meanwhile, OpenAI is in the headlines for two reasons. The company released a new keyboard designed for its coding app, but it is also facing a lawsuit from Apple. Apple alleges that OpenAI and a hardware firm led by Jony Ive were involved in trade secret theft, describing the business as rotten to its core.

In the semiconductor world, SK Hynix is making waves. The chip giant raised 26.5 billion dollars in a massive US share sale. The shares are scheduled to begin trading on the Nasdaq this Friday, marking the largest debut ever for a foreign company.

Elsewhere in business news, Chipotle is taking a bold step by opening its first restaurant in Mexico. It will be interesting to see how they perform, as other American fast food chains have found it difficult to succeed in the countries where their cuisine originated. In Europe, Gibraltar is entering a new era as 118-year-old border controls with Spain are finally removed. This change is expected to boost the local economy by allowing free movement.

Finally, a word of caution on consumer trends. The advertising watchdog has warned about portable air conditioners that claim to cool rooms in 90 seconds. These ads are misleading, and the devices do not perform as promised. On a more serious note, officials are investigating a frightening incident on a Ryanair flight where a passenger was nearly pulled out of a window mid-air. Fortunately, his wife was able to hold onto him until the situation was under control.
linkedin.cta: Get the full picture on the stories that matter to you at HEADLINNE.com.

instagram.title: Top 5 Things In Tech Today

slides.headline: A new name for Google's note app
slides.explanation: Google is rebranding its NotebookLM tool to Gemini Notebook. While the name is changing, it will still function as a standalone app while working more closely with other Google services.

slides.headline: Trump Media's new paid feed
slides.explanation: Trump Media is introducing a paid feed that gives Wall Street traders early access to key posts. This move aims to deliver influential updates faster to investors.

slides.headline: OpenAI's latest legal troubles
slides.explanation: Wired takes a deep dive into OpenAI's ongoing legal and reputation issues. The discussion focuses on how these challenges might impact the company's competition with Anthropic.

slides.headline: EU ruling on Google and Android
slides.explanation: The European Union has told Google to give AI competitors more access to its Android system. While it seems like a loss for Google, some analysts see it as a strategic win for the tech giant.

slides.headline: Wait, a ChatGPT basketball?
slides.explanation: OpenAI has released its first piece of physical hardware this week. Surprisingly, it is a basketball branded with ChatGPT, which has caught many people off guard.

instagram.caption: From rebranding apps to unexpected new hardware, it has been a busy week in the tech world. Swipe through to catch up on the five biggest stories you need to know today. Stay informed with Headlinne. Read more on HEADLINNE.com.

instagram.title: Top 5 Finance Stories Today

slides.headline: TSMC boosts US investment to $265 billion
slides.explanation: The chipmaker is committing another $100 billion to expand its American operations. They say this move will lead to more high-paying jobs in the tech sector.

slides.headline: Fed official hints at more rate hikes
slides.explanation: Dallas Fed President Lorie Logan says recent inflation data is not enough to stop interest rate increases. She believes rates should rise slightly to keep things in check.

slides.headline: White House staffer probed over betting claims
slides.explanation: A staffer is accused of using insider knowledge about presidential speeches to win nearly $100,000 in bets. The allegations involve trades made on the betting platform Kalshi.

slides.headline: Cybersecurity demand boosts Palo Alto stock
slides.explanation: Palo Alto Networks shares have doubled in three months. Investors are betting that the rise of AI will drive even more demand for the company's security products.

slides.headline: SpaceX faces big test after stock dip
slides.explanation: SpaceX stock just closed below its IPO price for the first time. All eyes are now on an upcoming rocket launch, which analysts say is the company's most important event right now.

instagram.caption: From major chip investments to shifting market trends, here is what you need to know in finance today. Stay updated with the biggest business headlines in just a few swipes. Get the full story on the Headlinne app. Read more on HEADLINNE.com.
